title: Platinum edition dvd set
wikitext:
[]: Platinum edition dvd set             Quote Post by mastervampire » Sun Jan 27, 2008 3:36 am I was looking on wikipedia and noticed in the notes section for a few episodes they had extra scenes added on the Platinum edition dvd set. i was just wondering since wikipedia didnt have pages for each episode, did every episode in the series have extra scenes added to them?

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sun Jan 27, 2008 3:40 am Just episodes 21-26 did if i remember correctly.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jan 27, 2008 3:46 am There were no extra scenes added to episode 25 and 26.

[]:             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Sun Jan 27, 2008 3:51 am Yeah. Just the Directors' Cuts of 21-24. And I was a little annoyed when I bought the thinpack boxed set that there's no Commentary of Doom or extra informational inserts like there are when you buy the original sized plantinum DVDs. Shock and dismay, that was.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sun Jan 27, 2008 3:55 am Damn...I ALWAYS forget EoTV.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Sat Feb 09, 2008 3:59 pm Is it worth buying the platinum edition since im currently still watching NGE on the internet or should i get the regular dvd's ???

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 09, 2008 4:17 pm Platinum has the DC versions of the later episodes, and better quality subs and video.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Sat Feb 09, 2008 4:24 pm Platinum it is then because i cant be bothered to buy DC separate , now all i need to do is find it in region 2 format for a reasonable price. Is the sound/picture quality that noticeable ???

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 09, 2008 4:42 pm Picture, yes. Not so sure about the sound (cloth ears). Also the subtitles fix a number of problems with the first time around.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 09, 2008 5:09 pm Renewal/Platinum doesn't have the original versions of 21 OA, 22 OA, and 23 DC, though. Piece of crap.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Feb 09, 2008 6:09 pm Reichu wrote: Renewal/Platinum doesn't have the original versions of 21 OA, 22 OA, and 23 DC, though. Piece of crap.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Sat Feb 09, 2008 6:45 pm ...original versions of 21/22 OA and 23 DC? What?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Feb 09, 2008 6:48 pm Shiro wrote: ...original versions of 21/22 OA and 23 DC? What?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Feb 09, 2008 11:04 pm Huh. So it only has it with the single DVDs? Guess I lucked out with that, then. I have the Single DVDs of Platinum and both the OA and DC episodes are on there. Shiro, can you confirm that with that set you have? (It's the metalbox version that you have, right?)

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Feb 10, 2008 12:08 am The metal box version supposedly has all the same content of the singles. It's only the platinum thinpak version that was missing all extras and on-air episodes of 21-24. So basically, if you got the thinpak, you were robbed.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Sun Feb 10, 2008 12:51 am No, I know that OA means on-air and DC means director's cut. But what's this about "original versions" of them? Yeah, the tin box has thinpak boxes, but it has the OA and DC episodes, as well as the commentaries, animatics and clean OPs. No booklets, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Feb 10, 2008 1:02 am If you get a hold of pre-Renewal/Plat copies of 21 OA, 22 OA, and 23 DC, and compare them to the Renewal/Plat versions, you'll find that Renewal/Plat does not provide straightforward remasterings; it has altered much of the footage.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Feb 10, 2008 2:23 am Reichu wrote: If you get a hold of pre-Renewal/Plat copies of 21 OA, 22 OA, and 23 DC, and compare them to the Renewal/Plat versions, you'll find that Renewal/Plat does not provide straightforward remasterings; it has altered much of the footage.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sun Feb 10, 2008 10:48 am Reichu wrote: If you get a hold of pre-Renewal/Plat copies of 21 OA, 22 OA, and 23 DC, and compare them to the Renewal/Plat versions, you'll find that Renewal/Plat does not provide straightforward remasterings; it has altered much of the footage.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Sun Feb 10, 2008 12:31 pm Yeah, no booklets in the tin box.
summary:
[]: Platinum edition dvd set             Quote Post by mastervampire » Sun Jan 27, 2008 3:36 am I was looking on wikipedia and noticed in the notes section for a few episodes they had extra scenes added on the Platinum edition dvd set. i was just wondering since wikipedia didnt have pages for each episode, did every episode in the series have extra scenes added to them?

[]:             Quot
